title: Background
text: crucial step single cell/nucleus RNAseq analysis implementation clustering algorithms generate groups cells/nuclei similar gene expression patterns. clusters often necessary classify observations (cells/nuclei) based cell state (cell type, developmental stage, etc.) unleash potential single cell/ nucleus approach. Clusters generated based patterns expression subset features, selection features highly influential downstream results. selecting features dimension reduction clustering, genes mitochondrial genome, known sex-linked genes, cell cycle genes (depending dataset) often removed consideration. “biological quality controls” can help produce clusters representing desired cell state removing expression patterns linked donor sex cell size. Since canonical cell state marker genes highly expressed one subset observations lowly expressed absent others, common feature selection methods identify genes highly variable expression within dataset. methods often rely modeling mean-variance relationship allow consideration potential batch effects models. title: Background
text: spatially resolved transcriptomics (SRT), individual observations single cells single nuclei, rather single points 2D space (tissue section). x- y-coordinates preserved sequencing, processed transcriptomic data can reconstruct gene expression patterns vary across different regions tissue present section. goal clustering SRT datasets often use gene expression patterns identify known spatial domains (e.g., different cortical layers brain). extra spatial dimension (x/y coordinates) SRT can capitalized upon identify features variable expression observations different regions tissue section (spatially variable genes SVGs). SVG selection methods often -perform classic single cell nuclei methods generating features recapitulate known spatial domains. However, SVG selection methods permit consideration user-defined batch effects may introduce technical noise sources variation may prevent distort spatial domains.

title: Background
text: analyzing 10X Visium SRT dataset human DLPFC, recognized need evaluate SVGs potential influence batch effects order optimize spatial domain clustering. used nnSVG select 2098 statistically significant SVGs used PRECAST identify clusters hopefully strongly corresponded cortical layers DLPFC. Using K=7 K=8, unable distinguish L5 L6 set features. also identified cluster observations appeared driven low UMI count rather expression SVGs. Figure 1.1 PRECAST clusters: 2098 input features (SVGs), K=7  Figure 1.2 PRECAST clusters: 2098 input features (SVGs), K=8  lack distinction L5 L6 appear due -representation known gene markers cortical layers SVG set. (DLPFC layer markers pulled Huuki-Meyers et. al, 2024 Table S8.) Figure 1.3 SVG set comprises known marker genes DLPFC layers. Nevertheless, supplemented SVG set significant markers cortical layer (top 100 lowest FDR layer), adding 327 features input clustering. found number L5 L6 markers increased mildly, indicating striking L5 L6 markers already included SVG list. Figure 1.4 Adding significant marker genes marginally increases SVG set. Supplementing SVGs additional layer markers improve spatial domain clustering PRECAST K=7 K=8. Figure 1.5 PRECAST clusters: 2425 input features (SVGs + layer markers), K=7 Figure 1.6 PRECAST clusters: 2425 input features (SVGs + layer markers), K=8 led us hypothesize small number features within SVG list capturing technical variation gene expression.
code: svg.df = read.csv("processed-data/svg_df.csv")  lm.df = read.csv("processed-data/TableS8_filtered-layer-markers.csv") is.lm = semi_join(lm.df[,c("gene","ensembl","domain_simple")],                    svg.df[,c("gene_name","gene_id")],                    by=c("gene"="gene_name","ensembl"="gene_id"))  not.lm = anti_join(distinct(svg.df[,c("gene_name","gene_id")]), is.lm,                     by=c("gene_name"="gene","gene_id"="ensembl")) %>%   mutate(domain_simple="none") %>% rename(gene=gene_name, ensembl=gene_id)  svg.lm.df = bind_rows(is.lm, not.lm) %>%    mutate(domain_simple=factor(domain_simple,                                levels=c("L1","L2","L3","L4","L5","L6","WM","none"))) ggplot(svg.lm.df, aes(x=domain_simple, fill=domain_simple))+   geom_bar(stat="count", width=.7, color="black")+   scale_fill_manual(values=c(cols_7[1:3],"white",cols_7[4:7]))+   labs(x="spatial domain",y="# SVGs", fill="")+   theme_bw() layer.markers.100 = read.csv("processed-data/TableS8_filtered-layer-markers.csv") %>%        group_by(test) %>% slice_min(n=100, fdr) lm100 = unique(layer.markers.100$ensembl) svg.lm.supp.df <- bind_rows(mutate(svg.lm.df, source="SVG"),            filter(lm.df, ensembl %in% setdiff(lm100, svg.lm.df$ensembl)) %>%             mutate(source="supp. LM") %>% select(gene,ensembl,domain_simple, source)) ggplot(svg.lm.supp.df, aes(x=domain_simple, fill=domain_simple,                             lty=factor(source, levels=c("supp. LM","SVG"),                                       labels=c("dashed","solid"))))+   geom_bar(stat="count", width=.7, color="black", position="stack")+   scale_linetype_identity()+   scale_fill_manual(values=c(cols_7[1:3],"white",cols_7[4:7]))+   labs(x="spatial domain",y="# SVGs", lty="source", fill="")+   theme_bw() c3 <- lapply(seq_along(spe_list), function(x) {   plotSpots(spe_list[[x]], annotate="clust_k7_svg.supp_layers", point_size=.1)+     scale_color_manual(values=cols_7_other2)+     labs(title=names(spe_list)[[x]])+     theme(legend.position="none", plot.margin=unit(c(0,0,0,0), units="cm")) }) grid.arrange(c3[[24]], c3[[19]], c3[[1]], c3[[10]], cols_7o2_legend(), ncol= 5) c4 <- lapply(seq_along(spe_list), function(x) {   plotSpots(spe_list[[x]], annotate="clust_k8_svg.supp_layers", point_size=.1)+     scale_color_manual(values=cols_8)+     labs(title=names(spe_list)[[x]])+     theme(legend.position="none", plot.margin=unit(c(0,0,0,0), units="cm")) }) grid.arrange(c4[[24]], c4[[19]], c4[[1]], c4[[10]], cols_8_legend(), ncol= 5) ### text excluded cause demonstration wasn't as strong as i remembered #The prior examples present the results of running PRECAST on all 24 samples in the dataset, with only 4 samples shown for simplicity. We observed that when PRECAST was run on different subsets of 8 samples, with the 4 example samples present in each subset, the resulting clusters varied for the samples present in both subsets. c5 <- lapply(seq_along(spe_list), function(x) {   plotSpots(spe_list[[x]], annotate="clust_k7_svg.raw_n8.1_layers", point_size=.1)+     scale_color_manual(values=cols_7_other)+     labs(title=names(spe_list)[[x]])+     theme(legend.position="none", plot.margin=unit(c(0,0,0,0), units="cm")) }) lay.mat = rbind(c(1,2,3,4,9),c(5,6,7,8,9)) grid.arrange(c5[[24]], c5[[19]], c5[[1]], c5[[10]],               c5[[2]], c5[[3]], c5[[4]], c5[[5]],              cols_7o_legend(), layout_matrix=lay.mat) c6 <- lapply(seq_along(spe_list), function(x) {   plotSpots(spe_list[[x]], annotate="clust_k7_svg.raw_n8.2_layers", point_size=.1)+     scale_color_manual(values=cols_7_other)+     labs(title=names(spe_list)[[x]])+     theme(legend.position="none", plot.margin=unit(c(0,0,0,0), units="cm")) }) #lay.mat = rbind(c(1,2,3,4,9),c(5,6,7,8,9)) grid.arrange(c6[[24]], c6[[19]], c6[[1]], c6[[10]],               c6[[20]], c6[[21]], c6[[22]], c6[[23]],              cols_7o_legend(), layout_matrix=lay.mat)

title: Find Biased Features
text: BiasDetect feature-based Quality Control (QC) bias genes identification spatial transcriptomics snRNA-seq data types batch effect. BiasDetect method based binomial deviance model (Townes et al, 2019) applies cutoffs based standard deviation (SD) deviance rank difference metrics data-driven thresholding approach find batch-biased features.

title: Find Biased Features
text: BiasDetect R package available Bioconductor version 3.19 later. can install BiasDetect using following commands R session Bioconductor: Additionally, can install development version GitHub: Install additional required packages running package codes vignettes.
code: if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install("BiasDetect")  ## Check Bioconductor installation BiocManager::valid() BiocManager::install("christinehou11/BiasDetect") pkgs <- c("dplyr", "tidyr", "scran", "scry", "nnSVG") required_pkgs <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())] BiocManager::install(required_pkgs)

title: Find Bias Genes — biasDetect
text: Function filter bias genes based selected selected threshold nSD deviance rank data frame plot.

title: Find Bias Genes — biasDetect
text: batch_df data.frame : Input data frame generated featureSelection() function using SpatialExperiment SingleCellExperiment object containing raw data. nSD_dev integer: Number standard deviation (nSD) deviance difference. default value 5. nSD_rank integer: Number standard deviation (nSD) rank difference. default value 5. visual logical: Whether display detected bias genes visualizations deviance rank. Default = FALSE return data frame. TRUE, returned format two parallel plots presenting bias genes based nSD_dev nSD_rank.

title: Find Bias Genes — biasDetect
text: output values identified bias genes. returned format can either data frame list two elements, giving option see either deviance rank plot.

title: Feature Selection — featureSelect
text: Function conduct feature selection snRNA-seq spatial transcriptomics data calculate difference deviance rank without selected batch effect.

title: Feature Selection — featureSelect
text: input SpatialExperiment SingleCellExperiment: Input data can either SpatialExperiment SingleCellExperiment. assumed assays slot containing counts assay devianceFeatureSelection() successfully operate calculate deviance rank. logcounts strongly recommended included assays. Also, input data assumed set rownames(input) genes gene_name rowData(input). batch_effect character: Either batch slide subject based metadata available within input data. name slide subject within input object specified based different scenarios. VGs character: Highly Variable Genes (HVGs) SingleCelleExperiment object Spatially Variable Genes (SVGs) SpatialExperiment object. data frame, assumed contain one column identified variable genes column name "gene_name".

title: Feature Selection — featureSelect
text: input provided SpatialExperiment SingleCellExperiment object, output values returned data frame containing deviance rank without bacth effect, corresponding difference, corresponding nSD, gene, gene name, whether gene outlier defined chosen deviance rank cutoff.

title: Feature Selection — featureSelect

title: Human Hippocampus . — spe
text: original data generated spatially-resolved transcriptomics (SRT) single-nucleus RNA-sequencing (snRNA-seq) data adjacent tissue sections anterior human hippocampus across ten adult neurotypical donors. SRT data generated using 10x Genomics Visium (n=36 capture areas) 10x Genomics Visium Spatial Proteogenomics (SPG) (n=8 capture areas). snRNA-seq data generated using 10x Genomics Chromium (n=26 total snRNA-seq libraries). original data processed using nnSVG() feature selection, spe_svgs dataset SpatialExperiment object containing 2098 spatially variable genes (SVGs).
